title: unona suberosa
intro: Unona suberosa, also known as the Bois Bandou or Bois d'arc, is a tropical plant native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins in its bark and leaves. The medicinal properties of Unona suberosa have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, showcasing its potential in providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Unona suberosa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a topical application for wound healing and skin care.
